response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with strong web/full-stack and systems credentials, but his profile does not match this role's core requirement of deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the last decade has centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practice, or industrial-protocol experience, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Strong low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang micro-services handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O and fibers)
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring and workshop/teaching track record
Broad 15+ year career spanning embedded, desktop, web, and event-driven geo-data platforms
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience (brief 2014–2015 role; C++ not even listed in skills)
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure, and no MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA protocol experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript/Node.js), suggesting a mismatch with an automation-machinery C++ role

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity/IEC 62443 and IoT protocol gap
